title: Dee Gives Birth

jokes.text: Look what Charlie and I found. Look at this. Oh, God. Ahh! Get that away from me. It reeks. What is that? Dude, this is our mail.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac excitedly showing something they found
jokes.punchline: It's their own mail, which is disgusting because it was in the sewer
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven absurdity. Charlie treating mail like treasure is perfectly in character and sets up the sewer subplot beautifully.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Look what Charlie and I found. Look at this.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, God. Ahh! Get that away from me. It reeks.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dude, this is our mail.

jokes.text: I threw it in there. You turkeys never open it, so I just started throwing it in the sewer.
jokes.setup: Why is their mail in the sewer?
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual admission that she's been throwing it there because they don't open it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee logic - lazy and inconsiderate but delivered with matter-of-fact confidence. 'You turkeys' is character-specific phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I threw it in there. You turkeys never open it, so I just started throwing it in the sewer.

jokes.text: Because if I threw it in the trash, you would find it and then yell at me. Besides, I certainly never expected you guys to be rooting around in the sewer.
jokes.setup: Why not throw mail in trash instead of sewer?
jokes.punchline: Dee's backwards logic that sewer is better than trash to avoid consequences
jokes.explanation: Solid character work showing Dee's self-serving rationalization. The logic is absurd but internally consistent for her.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Because if I threw it in the trash, you would find it and then yell at me. Besides, I certainly never expected you guys to be rooting around in the sewer.

jokes.text: We always go in the sewer. We hang out in the sewer. Why? There's stuff down there.
jokes.setup: What are Charlie and Mac doing in the sewer?
jokes.punchline: They casually reveal they regularly hang out in sewers like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurdity. Charlie and Mac's casual attitude about sewer exploration is perfectly in character and genuinely weird.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We always go in the sewer. We hang out in the sewer.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There's stuff down there.

jokes.text: Don't knock the sewer till you've tried the sewer. You find shit down there. But how are you so clean? We take our clothes off so we don't get dirty.
jokes.setup: Charlie defending sewer exploration
jokes.punchline: They go naked to avoid getting dirty while literally in a sewer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. The idea of being naked to stay clean while in a sewer is hilariously backwards. 'Don't knock the sewer till you've tried the sewer' is highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Don't knock the sewer till you've tried the sewer. You find shit down there.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: But how are you so clean?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We take our clothes off so we don't get dirty.

jokes.text: Big stuff. Rings, coins, nudity. That's part of the fun. The two of you are walking around in the sewers naked looking for rings and coins? You feel it with your feet. It's almost like clamming.
jokes.setup: What do they find in sewers?
jokes.punchline: Comparing naked sewer exploration to clamming
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie logic. The clamming comparison is so specific and wrong it's brilliant. 'Nudity' as something you find rather than something you do is perfect misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Big stuff. Rings, coins, nudity. That's part of the fun.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The two of you are walking around in the sewers naked looking for rings and coins?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You feel it with your feet. It's almost like clamming.

jokes.text: My water just broke! I'm having a baby.
jokes.setup: Extended argument about sewer exploration
jokes.punchline: Suddenly Dee is in labor, completely derailing the conversation
jokes.explanation: Classic narrative pivot. The timing and complete tonal shift from sewer talk to birth is expertly executed, though the line itself isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: My water just broke! I'm having a baby.

jokes.text: There's a baby inside of me running out of water. Look out!
jokes.setup: Dee's water broke
jokes.punchline: Her misunderstanding of what 'water breaking' means
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Dee's ignorance about pregnancy basics despite being pregnant. The urgency makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: There's a baby inside of me running out of water. Look out!

jokes.text: You hit 120! 120! I almost stewed my drawers. She was screaming in my ear. Probably screwed my transmission up.
jokes.setup: They rushed to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Dennis complaining about his car while Mac celebrates the reckless driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work. Dennis caring more about his car than the emergency and Mac being thrilled by danger is exactly right for them.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You hit 120! 120!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I almost stewed my drawers. She was screaming in my ear. Probably screwed my transmission up.

jokes.text: There's no guy? There's no guy. It's complicated. There's gotta be a guy. We'll go get the guy. We'll get the dad.
jokes.setup: Looking for the baby's father
jokes.punchline: Dee's evasiveness about there being no father figure
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot but isn't particularly funny on its own. The repetition of 'guy' has mild comedic effect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's no guy?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There's no guy.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It's complicated.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's gotta be a guy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We'll go get the guy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We'll get the dad.

jokes.text: Why don't you guys do what you do best and don't care, okay? I got it from here. We can take it from here. Right, Michael?
jokes.setup: Dee dismissing the guys
jokes.punchline: Accurately describing their usual behavior as 'not caring'
jokes.explanation: Good character insight delivered sarcastically. 'Do what you do best and don't care' is a solid burn that's unfortunately accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why don't you guys do what you do best and don't care, okay? I got it from here. We can take it from here. Right, Michael?

jokes.text: The brunt of the responsibility of this kid is gonna fall on us. We're gonna have to do everything. Look what she was doing with the mail, for Christ sakes.
jokes.setup: Realizing they'll be responsible for the baby
jokes.punchline: Using the mail-in-sewer incident as evidence of Dee's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the mail joke that reinforces the escalating panic about responsibility. Shows they learned nothing from their own sewer exploration.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The brunt of the responsibility of this kid is gonna fall on us. We're gonna have to do everything. Look what she was doing with the mail, for Christ sakes.

jokes.text: Look what this kid has ruined already. He's ruined my transmission. He's ruined a perfectly good sewer conversation.
jokes.setup: Listing what the unborn baby has already ruined
jokes.punchline: Charlie mourning the interrupted 'perfectly good sewer conversation'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work. Charlie valuing a sewer conversation as 'perfectly good' is hilariously wrong priorities. Great callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Look what this kid has ruined already. He's ruined my transmission.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's ruined a perfectly good sewer conversation.

jokes.text: Any multiple number of sperms could have gone up there and eaten the egg. There could be so many dads. No, sperm doesn't eat the egg. It doesn't eat the egg and grow strong and become a baby?
jokes.setup: Discussing how pregnancy works
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely wrong understanding of conception involving sperm eating eggs and growing strong
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie biology. The image of sperm 'eating' eggs and 'growing strong' is so wrong it's brilliant. Perfect character-specific ignorance.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Any multiple number of sperms could have gone up there and eaten the egg. There could be so many dads.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, sperm doesn't eat the egg.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It doesn't eat the egg and grow strong and become a baby?

jokes.text: Well, mostly because we're all on this side. You're over there. Just 'cause you guys made it to one side before me? You didn't make it to this side on time.
jokes.setup: Deciding who stays with Dee at the hospital
jokes.punchline: Using arbitrary physical positioning as the deciding factor
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy and character work showing their petty, arbitrary decision-making process. The unfairness is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, mostly because we're all on this side. You're over there. Just 'cause you guys made it to one side before me? You didn't make it to this side on time.

jokes.text: You are standing on top of a rolling chair. You're gonna go flying through that window, for God sakes. Get down.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to fix the TV
jokes.punchline: Pregnant Dee standing dangerously on a rolling chair near a window
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Dee's recklessness while pregnant. Dennis's concern is both genuine and exasperated.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You are standing on top of a rolling chair. You're gonna go flying through that window, for God sakes. Get down.

jokes.text: I have a very specific birth plan and mostly it involves me watching my stories to relax but I can't get this goddamn TV to do anything.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining her birth plan
jokes.punchline: Her 'specific' plan is just watching soap operas
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dee's shallow priorities. The contrast between 'very specific birth plan' and just watching TV is nicely done.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I have a very specific birth plan and mostly it involves me watching my stories to relax but I can't get this goddamn TV to do anything.

jokes.text: Have the baby first. Have the stupid baby. Then go flying through all the windows you like.
jokes.setup: Dennis frustrated with Dee's recklessness
jokes.punchline: Giving permission for window-flying after the birth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis's frustration. The casual permission to 'go flying through all the windows you like' after birth is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Have the baby first. Have the stupid baby. Then go flying through all the windows you like.

jokes.text: God, this baby is becoming such an irritating thing to me. Yeah. God, what an irritating thing babies are.
jokes.setup: Dennis dealing with pregnancy stress
jokes.punchline: Calling unborn baby an 'irritating thing' and generalizing about all babies
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's perfectly in character for Dennis. His irritation with an unborn baby is appropriately awful and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: God, this baby is becoming such an irritating thing to me. Yeah. God, what an irritating thing babies are.

jokes.text: How are we supposed to know this is a party if there's no beer? A party with no beer.
jokes.setup: Frank forgot beer for the interrogation party
jokes.punchline: Mac's confusion about what makes something a party
jokes.explanation: Functional comedy showing Mac's simple priorities. Not particularly clever but fits the setup for their scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: How are we supposed to know this is a party if there's no beer? A party with no beer.

jokes.text: We don't want them thinking it's an interrogation. Oh! Hey! Party! Party! All right! Hey!
jokes.setup: Trying to disguise interrogation as party
jokes.punchline: Cricket immediately acting like it's a party while Charlie just said they don't want that
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing. Cricket's enthusiasm immediately undermines their subtle approach, which is typical Cricket behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We don't want them thinking it's an interrogation.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Oh! Hey! Party! Party! All right! Hey!

jokes.text: Who invited Cricket? I did, because I figured it's a wild card. That's a stone you can leave unturned.
jokes.setup: Why is Cricket at the party?
jokes.punchline: Mac's mixed metaphor 'stone you can leave unturned'
jokes.explanation: Good Mac malapropism. Getting the phrase completely backwards while trying to sound wise is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Who invited Cricket? I did, because I figured it's a wild card. That's a stone you can leave unturned.

jokes.text: It's good to get away from the wife and kids for a little bit- let the hair down. So you're still with your wife after all that shit with Dee?
jokes.setup: Bill talking about normal guy time away from family
jokes.punchline: Immediately bringing up his affair with Dee
jokes.explanation: Good tonal shift from normal conversation to uncomfortable territory. The abruptness of Mac's question creates the cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: It's good to get away from the wife and kids for a little bit- let the hair down.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So you're still with your wife after all that shit with Dee?

jokes.text: You don't mind if I write some of this down, do you? For what? Just keeping a log of the party. We like to do that. A funny conversation pops up, you wanna remember it.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting to write things down
jokes.punchline: Obvious interrogation disguised as 'party logging'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. The obviousness of the ruse while trying to maintain the party facade creates awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You don't mind if I write some of this down, do you?

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Just keeping a log of the party. We like to do that. A funny conversation pops up, you wanna remember it.

jokes.text: I cannot stand the feel of a condom on my dick. I hate condoms. Hate 'em, hate 'em, hate 'em, hate 'em. The three of us just don't use 'em. How about you?
jokes.setup: Talking about 'guy stuff'
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to graphic condom discussion as obvious interrogation
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from their obvious leading questions. Mac's repeated 'hate 'em' and the transparent fishing for information works for the scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I cannot stand the feel of a condom on my dick. I hate condoms. Hate 'em, hate 'em, hate 'em, hate 'em. The three of us just don't use 'em. How about you?

jokes.text: That's why I had a vasectomy, guys. You had a vasectomy? Yep. You just winked when you said that. Did you have a vasectomy or not? No. But that's what I would tell the ladies so I didn't have to wear a condom.
jokes.setup: Bill claiming he had a vasectomy
jokes.punchline: Admitting he lies about vasectomies to avoid condoms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bill as a terrible person. The winking detail and immediate confession creates a nice comedic beat.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: That's why I had a vasectomy, guys.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You had a vasectomy?

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: Yep.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You just winked when you said that. Did you have a vasectomy or not?

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: No. But that's what I would tell the ladies so I didn't have to wear a condom.

jokes.text: If you do not get my sister her stories and a new room as soon as possible then I will come down on this hospital like the hammer of Thor. The thunder of my vengeance will echo through these corridors like the gust of a thousand winds!
jokes.setup: Dennis demanding help from hospital staff
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top Nordic god threats about TV shows
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis megalomaniac moment. The contrast between soap operas and Thor's hammer is perfect absurdist escalation. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If you do not get my sister her stories and a new room as soon as possible then I will come down on this hospital like the hammer of Thor. The thunder of my vengeance will echo through these corridors like the gust of a thousand winds!

jokes.text: You know what? I don't have time for this. I ain't going to jail over you. I will beat your ass and think nothing of it. I'm not doing this with you today. I'm not! I'm not!
jokes.setup: Dennis's over-the-top threats
jokes.punchline: Nurse immediately shutting him down with street threats
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dennis's pompous threats. The nurse's practical, no-nonsense response completely undermines his grandiosity.

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't have time for this. I ain't going to jail over you. I will beat your ass and think nothing of it. I'm not doing this with you today. I'm not! I'm not!

jokes.text: You left the hospital? Yeah. I was kind of hungry. Mostly I was just getting bored.
jokes.setup: Dee not in her room during labor
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting she left the hospital while in labor because she was bored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee irresponsibility. Leaving the hospital during labor for snacks shows her complete disconnect from the situation's seriousness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You left the hospital?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. I was kind of hungry. Mostly I was just getting bored.

jokes.text: Why is there a strange man in my room? They probably slid this man in here thinking that you were never gonna use the room. I don't want there to be a strange man laying in my room who looks dead.
jokes.setup: Finding someone else in Dee's hospital room
jokes.punchline: Hospital putting a possibly dead patient in the unused room
jokes.explanation: Good absurd hospital situation. The casual way they treat potentially dead patients as room-sharing is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why is there a strange man in my room?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: They probably slid this man in here thinking that you were never gonna use the room.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't want there to be a strange man laying in my room who looks dead.

jokes.text: I'm gonna scream in his face and see if he wakes up. Do not scream in the man's face. I'll jiggle his ankle, and that will determine whether he's alive or not.
jokes.setup: Checking if the man is alive
jokes.punchline: Dee's increasingly inappropriate methods of checking for life
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate behavior. Dee's casual approach to checking if someone is dead is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm gonna scream in his face and see if he wakes up.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Do not scream in the man's face.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'll jiggle his ankle, and that will determine whether he's alive or not.

jokes.text: Why would they put a dead old man with paper skin in my room when I'm trying to give birth?
jokes.setup: Finding a dead patient in her room
jokes.punchline: Dee's self-centered complaint about the inconvenience, describing 'paper skin'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Dee making the dead patient's presence about her inconvenience is perfectly selfish. 'Paper skin' is a nice grotesque detail.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why would they put a dead old man with paper skin in my room when I'm trying to give birth?

jokes.text: This was not part of my birth plan. This was not part of my birth plan, sir.
jokes.setup: Dead patient disrupting birth experience
jokes.punchline: Repeating birth plan complaint about corpse, even addressing the dead man
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the birth plan joke. Addressing the dead man directly about her birth plan is absurd and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: This was not part of my birth plan. This was not part of my birth plan, sir.

jokes.text: What's the deal here, bud? Well, no, she contacted me for a while usually when she was drunk. Drunken late-night booty calls? No. She usually just yells at me calls me names like army jerk, camo turkey.
jokes.setup: Asking about Dee's contact with soldier
jokes.punchline: Instead of booty calls, Dee just calls to insult him with specific military-themed names
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight. 'Army jerk' and 'camo turkey' are perfectly specific Dee insults. The subversion of booty calls into just verbal abuse is great.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's the deal here, bud?

dialogue.speaker: Soldier
dialogue.line: Well, no, she contacted me for a while usually when she was drunk.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Drunken late-night booty calls?

dialogue.speaker: Soldier
dialogue.line: No. She usually just yells at me calls me names like army jerk, camo turkey.

jokes.text: Sometimes she tricks me. She tricks you? She tricks you how? To give her sex. She's a very manipulative person.
jokes.setup: Asking if soldier slept with Dee
jokes.punchline: Soldier's innocent admission that Dee manipulates him into sex
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. The soldier's naive way of describing manipulation and abuse as 'tricking' is both sad and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Soldier
dialogue.line: Sometimes she tricks me.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She tricks you?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She tricks you how?

dialogue.speaker: Soldier
dialogue.line: To give her sex. She's a very manipulative person.

jokes.text: Are you guys mad at me? No, man, we're not mad. Pull it together, because you're a soldier and you should be tougher.
jokes.setup: Soldier getting emotional and asking if they're mad
jokes.punchline: Telling crying soldier to be tougher while claiming they're not mad
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mac and Charlie's inability to handle emotions while simultaneously criticizing the soldier for having them.

dialogue.speaker: Soldier
dialogue.line: Are you guys mad at me?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, man, we're not mad. Pull it together, because you're a soldier and you should be tougher.

jokes.text: This is the guy that's defending our country? Well, he's definitely a candidate.
jokes.setup: Soldier crying and being emotional
jokes.punchline: Questioning military capability based on emotional response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about military service. Charlie's understated 'definitely a candidate' is good deadpan response to Mac's concern.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is the guy that's defending our country?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, he's definitely a candidate.

jokes.text: What are your bridge friends doing here? You gotta give it the illusion of a party. It's gotta be a guy's night, that's why.
jokes.setup: Elderly people at the 'party'
jokes.punchline: Using bridge club seniors to create 'guy's night' illusion
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic. Frank's bridge club doesn't remotely fit 'guy's night' but he treats it like brilliant strategy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What are your bridge friends doing here?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You gotta give it the illusion of a party. It's gotta be a guy's night, that's why.

jokes.text: Are you high on blow? Whatever Duncan gave me. You don't even know what you're high on?
jokes.setup: Frank acting strange
jokes.punchline: Frank casually admitting he took unknown drugs from Duncan
jokes.explanation: Good Frank recklessness. Taking unknown drugs from a bridge partner and not caring what they are is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are you high on blow?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Whatever Duncan gave me.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You don't even know what you're high on?

jokes.text: What is L'il Kev doing here? You gotta throw out a big net to catch the fish that she was banging.
jokes.setup: Questioning why L'il Kev is at the party
jokes.punchline: Charlie's fishing metaphor for Dee's sexual partners
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that's both crude and somehow thoughtful. Charlie treating Dee's sex life like a fishing expedition is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What is L'il Kev doing here?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You gotta throw out a big net to catch the fish that she was banging.

jokes.text: Are you sure we're not taking this Weekend at Bernie's thing too far? I'm worried the sunglasses are ridiculous. The sunglasses lighten the mood. Make the whole thing feel like a fun, fun romp.
jokes.setup: Moving the dead patient
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating corpse disposal like a lighthearted movie reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference application. Dennis treating corpse disposal as a 'fun romp' by referencing Weekend at Bernie's is peak dark comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you sure we're not taking this Weekend at Bernie's thing too far? I'm worried the sunglasses are ridiculous. The sunglasses lighten the mood. Make the whole thing feel like a fun, fun romp.

jokes.text: Without the sunglasses Weekend at Bernie's would have been very dark. Yeah. Strange movie. Strange tale.
jokes.setup: Discussing Weekend at Bernie's logic
jokes.punchline: Treating a comedy about corpse manipulation as only 'strange' while literally doing it
jokes.explanation: Great meta commentary. Dennis analyzing Weekend at Bernie's as 'strange' while literally reenacting it is perfect ironic detachment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Without the sunglasses Weekend at Bernie's would have been very dark. Yeah. Strange movie. Strange tale.

jokes.text: What's our plan here? Are we just gonna throw him in the trash or find a laundry chute Fire him down that? Please don't put me in the trash.
jokes.setup: Discussing what to do with the patient
jokes.punchline: Patient weakly protesting being thrown in trash
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. The casual discussion of disposal methods followed by the patient's polite protest is brilliantly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What's our plan here? Are we just gonna throw him in the trash or find a laundry chute Fire him down that?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Craig
dialogue.line: Please don't put me in the trash.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh, are you alive? Oh, my goodness. What is going on here? What are you doing with Mr. Craig? They're trying to put me in the trash.
jokes.setup: Getting caught moving the patient
jokes.punchline: Mr. Craig immediately tattling about the trash plan
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the trash joke. Mr. Craig's immediate honest confession creates perfect consequences for Dennis's scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh, are you alive? Oh, my goodness. What is going on here? What are you doing with Mr. Craig?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Craig
dialogue.line: They're trying to put me in the trash.

jokes.text: Oh! It doesn't feel good. This sucks. This sucks a bag of dicks.
jokes.setup: Dee going into labor
jokes.punchline: Describing childbirth as 'sucking a bag of dicks'
jokes.explanation: Crude but in character for Dee. The phrase is memorable but not particularly clever, relying on shock value.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh! It doesn't feel good. This sucks. This sucks a bag of dicks.

jokes.text: Get your narrow ass back in that waiting room before I knock you in there. You have done enough today. Crazy-ass white boy steadily dressing up folk and talking about Thor.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to follow Dee to delivery
jokes.punchline: Nurse's specific callback to Dennis's Thor threats and corpse dressing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that perfectly summarizes Dennis's ridiculous day. 'Dressing up folk and talking about Thor' is a perfect encapsulation.

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: Get your narrow ass back in that waiting room before I knock you in there. You have done enough today. Crazy-ass white boy steadily dressing up folk and talking about Thor.

jokes.text: I used to have some jean shorts like that too. I slept in them shits, man. Eventually, I blew the crotch out of them things. But you can't wear 'em every day and expect for 'em to hold up. That was some nice-ass denim too. I miss them shorts. But you gotta take 'em off every now and then. You gotta take 'em off, son.
jokes.setup: Frank talking to someone about jean shorts
jokes.punchline: Detailed monologue about sleeping in and destroying jean shorts
jokes.explanation: Iconic Frank moment. The detailed nostalgia for disgusting jean shorts and the wisdom about taking them off is peak absurdist character comedy. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I used to have some jean shorts like that too. I slept in them shits, man. Eventually, I blew the crotch out of them things. But you can't wear 'em every day and expect for 'em to hold up. That was some nice-ass denim too. I miss them shorts. But you gotta take 'em off every now and then. You gotta take 'em off, son.

jokes.text: Why are you having sex with her? Well, she gets drunk and calls me a cardio-jerk or a workout turkey. And after a while, I just kind of cave in.
jokes.setup: Asking Rex about sleeping with Dee
jokes.punchline: Different military-themed insults leading to sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback expanding the pattern. 'Cardio-jerk' and 'workout turkey' continue Dee's specific insult vocabulary. The cycle of abuse leading to sex is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why are you having sex with her?

dialogue.speaker: Rex
dialogue.line: Well, she gets drunk and calls me a cardio-jerk or a workout turkey. And after a while, I just kind of cave in.

jokes.text: She's just berating these guys into having sex with her. Yeah, and she is slammin' ass all over town. She's getting way more action than us. She's stuck on her curse words. 'Jerk' and 'turkey' are all that she's using.
jokes.setup: Analyzing Dee's pattern with men
jokes.punchline: Charlie's observation about her limited insult vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Great character analysis. Charlie's linguistic observation about Dee's limited curse vocabulary is both funny and accurate to the pattern.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She's just berating these guys into having sex with her.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, and she is slammin' ass all over town.

dialogue.line: She's stuck on her curse words. 'Jerk' and 'turkey' are all that she's using.

jokes.text: Kids spend all their time on the Internet. 'Hi. Who are you? There's your penis and there's your butt.' You gotta learn from some books. You don't want 'em reading too many books. You don't want 'em to be a nerd.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to raise kids
jokes.punchline: Mac's naive view of internet dangers followed by anti-book stance
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mac's backwards priorities. Warning about internet while being anti-education is perfectly contradictory.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Kids spend all their time on the Internet. 'Hi. Who are you? There's your penis and there's your butt.' You gotta learn from some books. You don't want 'em reading too many books. You don't want 'em to be a nerd.

jokes.text: If it's a nerd, I'm gonna bash his head in. Nerds get nowhere in life. Oh, my God. Is he a nerd? I could bash some nerds right now. Right after this we should probably bash nerds. I gotta get it out of my system.
jokes.setup: Discussing not wanting a nerd child
jokes.punchline: Escalating to immediate violence against nerds in general
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mac and Charlie's violent stupidity. The progression from hypothetical to immediate action is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If it's a nerd, I'm gonna bash his head in. Nerds get nowhere in life. Oh, my God. Is he a nerd? I could bash some nerds right now. Right after this we should probably bash nerds. I gotta get it out of my system.

jokes.text: We have a lot of ideas about raising a kid. We are men who can be great dads. We have great opinions. And that's all that parenting is - pretending you know what you're talking about and then jamming it down a kid's throat.
jokes.setup: Feeling confident about parenting
jokes.punchline: Defining parenting as pretending and force-feeding opinions
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark insight into their mindset. The definition of parenting as authoritarian force-feeding is both wrong and honestly what they would do.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We have a lot of ideas about raising a kid. We are men who can be great dads. We have great opinions.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: And that's all that parenting is - pretending you know what you're talking about and then jamming it down a kid's throat.

jokes.text: Charlie and I have decided that we are going to be the father of this child. Dude, we did a complete 180.
jokes.setup: Announcing their decision to Dennis
jokes.punchline: Claiming a '180' when they just went from not wanting responsibility to wanting control
jokes.explanation: Good character work. They think wanting control instead of avoiding responsibility is growth, which is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie and I have decided that we are going to be the father of this child.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, we did a complete 180.

jokes.text: I did this Weekend at Bernie's operation earlier that would have blown you guys' minds - it was very effective. Then I busted out this sweet Thor line on this lady because she was pissing me off. It was, like, boom! I really hit her with it. And I never reference the Nordic gods.
jokes.setup: Dennis sharing his day's accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Bragging about corpse disposal and Nordic god references as achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism. Bragging about moving corpses and mythological references as personal growth is exactly his character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I did this Weekend at Bernie's operation earlier that would have blown you guys' minds - it was very effective. Then I busted out this sweet Thor line on this lady because she was pissing me off. It was, like, boom! I really hit her with it. And I never reference the Nordic gods.

jokes.text: Then I busted out this sweet Thor line on this lady because she was pissing me off. It was, like, boom! I really hit her with it. And I never reference the Nordic gods.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing to brag
jokes.punchline: Proud of his ridiculous Norse god threats
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Dennis genuinely proud of his ridiculous behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Then I busted out this sweet Thor line on this lady because she was pissing me off. It was, like, boom! I really hit her with it. And I never reference the Nordic gods.

jokes.text: Maybe instead of doing a My Two Dads type thing we could fold in another dad and do a Three Men and a Baby type situation. That's what it is. I like that. An equally effective movie.
jokes.setup: Figuring out their parenting arrangement
jokes.punchline: Using 80s comedy movies as parenting templates
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about using movie formulas for real life. Their confidence in '80s comedies as parenting guides is absurd and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Maybe instead of doing a My Two Dads type thing we could fold in another dad and do a Three Men and a Baby type situation.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's what it is.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I like that.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: An equally effective movie.

jokes.text: I couldn't decide who the dad was, so I brought everybody. You didn't try and decide who the dad was. You just got high. Well, the interrogation did turn into a coke party.
jokes.setup: Frank bringing everyone to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Admitting the investigation became a drug party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic. The casual admission that an investigation became a cocaine party is exactly his approach to problem-solving.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I couldn't decide who the dad was, so I brought everybody.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You didn't try and decide who the dad was. You just got high.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, the interrogation did turn into a coke party.

jokes.text: If this kid is mine, I'm gonna blow my brains out. Oh, he got zooted, man. He took an eight-ball to the dome.
jokes.setup: Bill's reaction to potential fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Suicidal ideation followed by casual drug commentary
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about fatherhood fear, though the suicide joke feels more shock value than earned. Duncan's drug commentary adds absurd contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: If this kid is mine, I'm gonna blow my brains out.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Oh, he got zooted, man. He took an eight-ball to the dome.

jokes.text: We're gonna be the father of the baby now. We're gonna be the fathers. It has been decided, so sayeth Thor. Thor said it. The who? Thor. Who Thor? Is that your Greek God reference? He's a Nordic God.
jokes.setup: Dennis invoking Thor again
jokes.punchline: Frank's confusion about Thor's mythology
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Thor reference. Frank's mythological confusion and Dennis's correction work well together.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're gonna be the father of the baby now. We're gonna be the fathers. It has been decided, so sayeth Thor. Thor said it.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The who?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Thor.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Who Thor?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Is that your Greek God reference?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's a Nordic God.

jokes.text: Carmen? What are you doing here? I'm here to get my baby. What? Oh, yeah. The tranny's the dad. Yeah!
jokes.setup: Unexpected appearance of Carmen at hospital
jokes.punchline: Revealing Carmen is the actual father, not any of the suspects
jokes.explanation: Strong plot twist that subverts all their investigation. The reveal works well structurally, though the language used is dated.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Carmen? What are you doing here?

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: I'm here to get my baby.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The tranny's the dad. Yeah!

jokes.text: No. I was their surrogate. We used her sperm and a donor egg and then they just kind of - right up there and - We had a difficult time finding a surrogate, but Dee was a miracle to us.
jokes.setup: Confusion about the baby's parentage
jokes.punchline: Dee was just a surrogate, not the mother, explaining the whole situation
jokes.explanation: Good resolution that explains the mystery. Dee's awkward explanation and Carmen's genuine gratitude provide nice contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: No. I was their surrogate. We used her sperm and a donor egg and then they just kind of - right up there and -

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: We had a difficult time finding a surrogate, but Dee was a miracle to us.

jokes.text: Dee, you tricked us? How did I trick you? We were getting all pumped up about being this baby's dad. We were all keyed up for nothing.
jokes.setup: Dennis feeling betrayed about the surrogate situation
jokes.punchline: Being upset about not getting to impose their will on a child
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing their selfishness. Being disappointed about not controlling a child is perfectly awful for them.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee, you tricked us?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How did I trick you?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We were getting all pumped up about being this baby's dad. We were all keyed up for nothing.

jokes.text: We were gonna impose our will on the kid. We had a Three Men and a Baby thing going on.
jokes.setup: Explaining what they lost
jokes.punchline: Openly stating plans to 'impose their will' on child
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes their parenting philosophy explicit. 'Impose our will' is honestly what they wanted to do, which is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We were gonna impose our will on the kid. We had a Three Men and a Baby thing going on.

jokes.text: So, what I'm confused on is the mother is the father and the father is black And the baby's not black, so - It's all been explained. Yeah. I'll talk you through it later.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to understand the surrogacy situation
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled understanding of the arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good Frank confusion. His attempt to understand surrogacy through completely wrong categories is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: So, what I'm confused on is the mother is the father and the father is black And the baby's not black, so -

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's all been explained.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'll talk you through it later.

jokes.text: What is your obsession with the sewer these days? You find great stuff down there. Like what? Like sewage. Ha-ha! Good one, Dee. She's back, baby. She's back.
jokes.setup: Dee questioning Charlie's sewer obsession
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sincere answer 'sewage' and Mac celebrating Dee's sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the sewer subplot. Charlie's honest answer about finding sewage and Mac thinking Dee made a joke is perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What is your obsession with the sewer these days?

dialogue.line: You find great stuff down there.

dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Like sewage.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Ha-ha! Good one, Dee. She's back, baby. She's back.

jokes.text: Supposing it's storming and a guy loses his ring And it goes into the gutter? You gotta come up with something else, Frank. You can't keep using the same - Like, give me some crack. You know what I'm sayin', dawg?
jokes.setup: Frank trying to explain sewer exploration again
jokes.punchline: Duncan interrupting to ask for crack cocaine
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing Duncan's priorities. The interruption of Frank's ring theory with a direct drug request is appropriately absurd.

dialogue.line: Supposing it's storming and a guy loses his ring And it goes into the gutter?

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Like, give me some crack. You know what I'm sayin', dawg?

tmdb_overview: Dee's blessed event is fast approaching but the father's identity remains a mystery---to the gang, at least. So they throw a party and invite all of Dee's former flings.
